content: Treat standard agent communication protocols as a fixed 'context budget' analogous to the limited memory budget that memory-efficient intrinsic curiosity module (ICM) implementations optimize for large-scale autonomous agent swarms. This allows adapting memory compression techniques from sparse ICM design to prune redundant, low-signal inter-agent messages, keeping only high-information communication that preserves collective task performance without saturating available network or agent-side context capacity.
source_topics: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
